title: Comfort overlays
text: default comfort model ISO 7730 PMV. geom_comfort_overlay() draws filled PMV bands, scale_fill_comfort_pmv() applies PMV-centered diverging palette, geom_comfort_pmv_lines() adds labelled PMV curves.
code: ggpsychro(tdb_lim = c(5, 40), hum_lim = c(0, 24)) +     psychro_preset("minimal") +     geom_comfort_overlay(n = c(70, 48), gap = 0) +     scale_fill_comfort_pmv(name = "PMV") +     geom_comfort_pmv_lines(levels = seq(-3, 3, by = 0.5), n = 140)

title: Comfort overlays
text: PMV-based standards, geom_comfort_standard_zone() draws filled zone, PMV boundary curves, labels standard helper.
code: ggpsychro(tdb_lim = c(5, 35), hum_lim = c(0, 24)) +     psychro_preset("minimal") +     geom_comfort_standard_zone(comfort_standard_ashrae55_2017(), n = 140) ggpsychro(tdb_lim = c(5, 35), hum_lim = c(0, 24)) +     psychro_preset("minimal") +     geom_comfort_standard_zone(comfort_standard_en15251_2007(), n = 140)

title: Comfort overlays
text: Create reusable model objects comfort_model_*() helpers. SET overlays default "set" metric, adaptive models default "acceptability". continuous metrics SET, use filled bands labelled contours field remains visible without losing level boundaries. geom_comfort_contour(label = TRUE) draws contour inline level label one layer, leaving gap contour label placed.   Adaptive comfort zones temperature bands rather humidity-dependent fields, filled adaptive zone expected look like vertical band instead curved humidity field. example , t_running = 20 gives ASHRAE 55 80% acceptability interval 20.5 27.5 degrees C; tr defaults tdb, zone clipped current chart limits saturation boundary.
code: set_model <- comfort_model_set()  ggpsychro(tdb_lim = c(15, 30), hum_lim = c(0, 20)) +     psychro_preset("minimal") +     geom_comfort_overlay(         model = set_model,         metric = "set",         levels = seq(14, 32, by = 2),         n = c(70, 42),         alpha = 0.32     ) +     scale_fill_viridis_c("SET (deg C)", option = "C") +     geom_comfort_contour(         model = set_model,         metric = "set",         breaks = seq(22, 30, by = 2),         n = c(70, 42),         label = TRUE,         label_size = 3,         colour = "#2F2F2F",         linewidth = 0.7     ) adaptive_model <- comfort_model_adaptive(t_running = 20)  ggpsychro(tdb_lim = c(15, 30), hum_lim = c(0, 20)) +     psychro_preset("minimal") +     geom_comfort_zone(         model = adaptive_model,         fill = "#22c55e",         alpha = 0.18,         colour = "#166534",         linewidth = 0.7     )

title: Comfort overlays
text: Heat Index overlays use comfort_model_heat_index() geom_comfort_heat_index() draw Outdoor Work Heat Index categories. model uses NOAA-style Heat Index calculation internally Fahrenheit, layer follows parent chart’s unit system.
code: ggpsychro(tdb_lim = c(20, 45), hum_lim = c(0, 35)) +     psychro_preset("minimal") +     geom_comfort_heat_index(n = c(70, 42), alpha = 0.5)

title: Comfort overlays
text: Givoni overlays strategy zones rather single continuous comfort metric. Create strategy comfort_strategy_givoni() draw geom_comfort_givoni(). mean outdoor temperature shifts strategy geometry shown chart dashed marker.  Use zone_style element_comfort_zone() highlight individual strategy zones without changing strategy geometry.
code: givoni <- comfort_strategy_givoni(mean_outdoor = 22)  ggpsychro(tdb_lim = c(-10, 50), hum_lim = c(0, 35)) +     psychro_preset("minimal") +     geom_comfort_givoni(givoni, alpha = 0.45) ggpsychro(tdb_lim = c(-10, 50), hum_lim = c(0, 35)) +     psychro_preset("minimal") +     geom_comfort_givoni(         givoni,         zone_style = list(             comfort = element_comfort_zone(                 fill = "#66D27A", colour = "#1F5F2D", alpha = 0.35             ),             natural_ventilation = element_comfort_zone(                 fill = "#B6E3FF", colour = "#2F6FB0", alpha = 0.25             ),             winter = element_comfort_zone(                 colour = "#A14D00", linetype = "dashed"             ),             air_conditioning = element_comfort_zone(                 colour = "#8B1E3F", linewidth = 1.2             )         )     )

title: Comfort overlays
text: stat_comfort_state() evaluates model supplied psychrometric states. computed comfort fields can used after_stat().
code: states <- data.frame(     tdb = c(22, 24, 26, 28),     relhum = c(45, 50, 55, 60) )  ggpsychro(states, tdb_lim = c(15, 32), hum_lim = c(0, 22)) +     psychro_preset("minimal") +     stat_comfort_state(         aes(tdb = tdb, relhum = relhum, colour = after_stat(pmv)),         size = 3     ) +     scale_colour_gradient2(         "PMV",         low = "#3B5FFF",         mid = "#4A4A4A",         high = "#FF3B30",         midpoint = 0     )

title: Comfort overlays
text: Comfort layers follow parent chart’s coordinate system. Use mollier = TRUE Mollier orientation units = "IP" IP inputs.  Use manual psychrometric zones desired region project-specific operating envelope rather thermal comfort model. See Zones processes layers.
code: ggpsychro(tdb_lim = c(15, 30), hum_lim = c(0, 20), mollier = TRUE) +     psychro_preset("minimal") +     geom_comfort_overlay(n = c(50, 30)) +     scale_fill_comfort_pmv(name = "PMV") +     geom_comfort_pmv_lines(levels = seq(-2, 2, by = 1), n = 100)

title: Get started
text: tdb_lim controls dry-bulb temperature range. hum_lim controls displayed humidity ratio range. SI units used default.  Use units = "IP" IP charts. dry-bulb limits degree F humidity-ratio limits grains per pound dry air.
code: ggpsychro(tdb_lim = c(10, 35), hum_lim = c(0, 25), units = "SI") ggpsychro(tdb_lim = c(32, 122), hum_lim = c(0, 220), units = "IP")

title: Get started
text: Set mollier = TRUE swap chart Mollier orientation.
code: ggpsychro(tdb_lim = c(0, 50), hum_lim = c(0, 30), mollier = TRUE)

title: Get started
text: ggpsychro() sets psychrometric coordinate system . building plot smaller pieces, coord_psychro() can also added explicitly.
code: ggpsychro() +     coord_psychro(tdb_lim = c(10, 35), hum_lim = c(0, 25), units = "SI")

title: Get started
text: result ggplot object, regular ggplot layers work usual.  data recorded relative humidity, wet-bulb temperature, vapor pressure, specific volume, enthalpy, see Plotting psychrometric data.
code: states <- data.frame(     dry_bulb_temperature = c(18, 23, 28, 31),     humidity_ratio = c(8, 9.5, 10.5, 13) )  ggpsychro(states, aes(dry_bulb_temperature, humidity_ratio),     tdb_lim = c(0, 40), hum_lim = c(0, 25) ) +     psychro_preset("minimal") +     geom_point(color = "#0f766e", size = 2)

title: Chart grids and styles
text: psychro_preset() applies named collection scales, labelled reference grids, matching theme. built-presets "ashrae" "minimal".   Use labels = FALSE want preset’s grid visibility theme grid labels.
code: ggpsychro(tdb_lim = c(0, 50), hum_lim = c(0, 30)) +     psychro_preset("ashrae") ggpsychro(tdb_lim = c(0, 50), hum_lim = c(0, 40)) +     psychro_preset("minimal") ggpsychro(tdb_lim = c(0, 50), hum_lim = c(0, 30)) +     psychro_preset("ashrae", labels = FALSE)

title: Chart grids and styles
text: reference grid helpers :  helpers add data layers. update psychrometric coordinate metadata panel background can draw hide selected grid.
code: ggpsychro(tdb_lim = c(0, 50), hum_lim = c(0, 40)) +     geom_grid_relhum() +     geom_grid_wetbulb() +     geom_grid_vappres() +     geom_grid_specvol() +     geom_grid_enthalpy()

title: Chart grids and styles
text: reference grid matching scale_*_continuous() function. Use scales customize breaks, minor breaks, limits, labels.  Line style arguments go directly ggplot2::element_line(). Label style arguments use label. prefix, label.size, label.color, label.vjust.
code: ggpsychro(tdb_lim = c(0, 50), hum_lim = c(0, 40)) +     geom_grid_relhum(linewidth = 0.8, color = "black", label.size = 5) +     scale_relhum_continuous(breaks = seq(25, 75, by = 25), minor_breaks = NULL) +     geom_grid_wetbulb(color = "black", linewidth = 0.6, label = FALSE) +     scale_wetbulb_continuous(breaks = seq(5, 30, by = 5), minor_breaks = NULL) +     geom_grid_vappres(show = FALSE) +     scale_specvol_continuous(labels = NULL) +     geom_grid_enthalpy()

title: Chart grids and styles
text: geom_psychro_protractor() adds ASHRAE-style sensible heat ratio heat-moisture-ratio protractor chart mask area. parent chart decides orientation: regular charts place upper-left mask area, Mollier charts rotate protractor lower-right mask area.  Use scale resize whole protractor length-2 margin adjust horizontal vertical mask-area offsets. Tick breaks labels controlled guide_psychro_protractor().
code: ggpsychro(tdb_lim = c(0, 50), hum_lim = c(0, 30)) +     psychro_preset("ashrae") +     geom_psychro_protractor() ggpsychro(tdb_lim = c(0, 50), hum_lim = c(0, 30), mollier = TRUE) +     psychro_preset("minimal") +     geom_psychro_protractor(         scale = 0.85,         margin = c(0.06, 0.12),         guide = guide_psychro_protractor(             shr_breaks = c(0, 0.2, 0.4, 0.6, 0.8, 1),             shr_minor_breaks = seq(0.1, 0.9, by = 0.1),             ratio_labels = NULL         )     )

title: Chart grids and styles
text: ggpsychro adds psychrometric theme elements panel masks reference grids. Use regular theme() calls.
code: ggpsychro(tdb_lim = c(0, 50), hum_lim = c(0, 30)) +     geom_grid_relhum(label = FALSE) +     theme(         psychro.panel.grid.saturation = element_line(             color = "black", linetype = 2         ),         psychro.panel.mask = element_polygon(fill = "gray90", color = NA)     )

title: Plotting psychrometric data
text: Use stat = "relhum" regular ggplot geom row dry-bulb temperature relative humidity. Relative humidity values supplied percent values.
code: weather <- data.frame(     month = rep(c("May", "June", "July", "August"), each = 12),     hour = rep(seq(0, 22, by = 2), times = 4) ) weather$dry_bulb_temperature <- 16 +     rep(c(1, 4, 7, 6), each = 12) +     9 * sin((weather$hour - 6) / 24 * 2 * pi) weather$relative_humidity <- 72 -     rep(c(0, 8, 14, 10), each = 12) -     18 * sin((weather$hour - 6) / 24 * 2 * pi) weather$relative_humidity <- pmax(30, pmin(95, weather$relative_humidity))  ggpsychro(weather, tdb_lim = c(0, 40), hum_lim = c(0, 25)) +     psychro_preset("minimal") +     geom_point(         aes(dry_bulb_temperature, relhum = relative_humidity),         stat = "relhum",         color = "#0f766e",         alpha = 0.55,         size = 1.6     ) +     facet_wrap(~month)

title: Plotting psychrometric data
text: stats can used line geoms. useful adding calculated reference lines overlays data.
code: ggpsychro(tdb_lim = c(10, 35), hum_lim = c(0, 25)) +     geom_grid_relhum() +     geom_grid_wetbulb() +     geom_line(         aes(x = 20:32, wetbulb = 18),         stat = "wetbulb",         color = "#be123c",         linewidth = 1     )

title: Plotting psychrometric data
text: property-specific stats : stat also needs dry-bulb temperature x. used inside ggpsychro() plot, units pressure inherited parent chart.
code: line_data <- data.frame(tdb = seq(15, 35, by = 1))  ggpsychro(tdb_lim = c(10, 40), hum_lim = c(0, 30)) +     psychro_preset("minimal") +     geom_line(         aes(x = tdb, relhum = 50),         data = line_data,         stat = "relhum",         color = "#0f766e"     ) +     geom_line(         aes(x = tdb, wetbulb = 18),         data = line_data,         stat = "wetbulb",         color = "#be123c"     ) #> Warning: Computation failed in `stat_wetbulb()`. #> Caused by error in `GetHumRatioFromTWetBulb()`: #> ! Wet bulb temperature is above dry bulb temperature

title: Plotting psychrometric data
text: Hourly weather simulation outputs can summarized psychrometric tiles. geom_psychro_tile() bins observations dry-bulb temperature humidity ratio coordinates. accepts either humidity ratio y relative humidity relhum, exposes after_stat(hours) distribution plots.  bins can aggregate another metric value aesthetic fun.  model-based thermal comfort overlays, see Comfort overlays. manual state points, process lines, zones, see Zones processes.
code: weather$cooling_load <- pmax(0, weather$dry_bulb_temperature - 24) * 1.5  ggpsychro(weather, tdb_lim = c(0, 40), hum_lim = c(0, 25)) +     geom_grid_relhum() +     geom_psychro_tile(         aes(             dry_bulb_temperature,             relhum = relative_humidity,             fill = after_stat(hours)         ),         binwidth = c(2, 1)     ) +     scale_fill_gradient("Hours", low = "#dbeafe", high = "#1d4ed8") ggpsychro(weather, tdb_lim = c(0, 40), hum_lim = c(0, 25)) +     geom_grid_relhum() +     geom_psychro_tile(         aes(             dry_bulb_temperature,             relhum = relative_humidity,             value = cooling_load,             fill = after_stat(value)         ),         binwidth = c(4, 2),         fun = "mean"     ) +     scale_fill_gradient("Mean cooling load", low = "#fef3c7", high = "#b45309")

title: Zones and processes
text: geom_psychro_zone() draws filled psychrometric regions. default zone type, "dbt-rh", uses dry-bulb relative-humidity limits.
code: zones <- rbind(     data.frame(         name = "comfort",         tdb_min = 20, tdb_max = 26,         relhum_min = 35, relhum_max = 60     ),     data.frame(         name = "humid",         tdb_min = 24, tdb_max = 32,         relhum_min = 60, relhum_max = 85     ) )  ggpsychro(tdb_lim = c(0, 40), hum_lim = c(0, 25)) +     psychro_preset("minimal") +     geom_psychro_zone(         aes(             tdb_min = tdb_min, tdb_max = tdb_max,             relhum_min = relhum_min, relhum_max = relhum_max,             fill = name         ),         data = zones,         type = "dbt-rh",         alpha = 0.28,         colour = NA     )

title: Zones and processes
text: geom_psychro_process() draws path state points. Supply tdb plus exactly one state property: humratio, relhum, wetbulb, vappres, specvol, enthalpy. Relative humidity supplied percent values.
code: process <- data.frame(     stage = c("return", "mixed", "cooled", "supply"),     dry_bulb_temperature = c(28, 24, 14, 18),     relative_humidity = c(55, 60, 95, 55) )  ggpsychro(tdb_lim = c(0, 40), hum_lim = c(0, 25)) +     psychro_preset("minimal") +     geom_psychro_process(         aes(tdb = dry_bulb_temperature, relhum = relative_humidity),         data = process,         colour = "#0f766e",         linewidth = 1,         arrow = grid::arrow(length = grid::unit(0.08, "inches"))     ) +     stat_psychro_state(         aes(tdb = dry_bulb_temperature, relhum = relative_humidity),         data = process,         colour = "#0f766e",         size = 2     )

title: Zones and processes
text: Zones can also bounded enthalpy relative humidity, specific volume relative humidity, dry-bulb plus maximum humidity ratio, explicit polygon points.  Use type = "xy-points" desired region already known polygon points dry-bulb temperature display humidity-ratio units.
code: limits <- data.frame(     enthalpy_min = 35000,     enthalpy_max = 65000,     relhum_min = 30,     relhum_max = 70 )  ggpsychro(tdb_lim = c(0, 40), hum_lim = c(0, 25)) +     psychro_preset("minimal") +     geom_psychro_zone(         aes(             enthalpy_min = enthalpy_min, enthalpy_max = enthalpy_max,             relhum_min = relhum_min, relhum_max = relhum_max         ),         data = limits,         type = "enthalpy-rh",         fill = "#a855f7",         alpha = 0.22,         colour = NA     ) polygon_zone <- data.frame(     tdb = c(18, 27, 30, 21),     humratio = c(6, 7, 12, 11) )  ggpsychro(tdb_lim = c(0, 40), hum_lim = c(0, 25)) +     psychro_preset("minimal") +     geom_psychro_zone(         aes(tdb = tdb, humratio = humratio),         data = polygon_zone,         type = "xy-points",         fill = "#f97316",         alpha = 0.24,         colour = NA     )

title: A ggplot2 Extension for Making Psychrometric Charts
text: ggplot2-native psychrometric charts SI/IP units Mollier orientation. Preset grid style helpers common psychrometric chart layouts. ASHRAE-style psychrometric protractor sensible heat ratio heat-moisture-ratio guides chart mask area. Psychrometric stats relative humidity, wet-bulb temperature, vapor pressure, specific volume, enthalpy, humidity ratio. State points, process lines, filled operating zones HVAC workflows. Thermal comfort bioclimatic overlays PMV/PPD, SET, adaptive comfort, labelled contours, Heat Index, PMV-based ASHRAE 55 / EN 15251 zones, Givoni strategy zones.

title: A ggplot2 Extension for Making Psychrometric Charts
text: creation psychrometric chart starts ggpsychro(). result ggplot object, grids, stats, geoms, scales, themes can added + workflow used ggplot2.  Comfort overlays can added regular layers. PMV overlay combines root-traced filled bands, PMV isolines, text labels psychrometric chart.
code: ggpsychro(tdb_lim = c(0, 50), hum_lim = c(0, 30)) +     psychro_preset("minimal") ggpsychro(tdb_lim = c(5, 40), hum_lim = c(0, 24)) +     psychro_preset("minimal") +     geom_comfort_overlay(n = c(70, 48), gap = 0) +     scale_fill_comfort_pmv(name = "PMV") +     geom_comfort_pmv_lines(levels = seq(-3, 3, by = 0.5), n = 140)

title: A ggplot2 Extension for Making Psychrometric Charts
text: longer examples live pkgdown site: Get started - build basic charts, choose limits units, use Mollier orientation, add ggplot layers. Chart grids styles - tune dry-bulb, humidity-ratio, relative-humidity, wet-bulb, enthalpy, specific-volume grid guides, plus psychrometric protractor. Plotting psychrometric data - plot points, bins, overlays weather measured state data. Comfort overlays - draw PMV, SET, adaptive comfort, labelled comfort contours, Heat Index, PMV-based standard zones, Givoni bioclimatic strategy overlays. Zones processes - draw manual comfort regions, operating limits, state points, HVAC process lines.

title: A ggplot2 Extension for Making Psychrometric Charts
text: ggpsychro’s recent chart-layout higher-level layer work informed two excellent psychrometric chart projects: psychrochart, especially preset-oriented chart configuration, labelled reference grids, zone support, export-focused chart composition. Andrew Marsh’s Psychrometric Chart, especially interactive psychrometric workflows, weather/data overlays, comfort display ideas, import/export-oriented chart interactions. ggpsychro vendor code projects. implementation remains R/ggplot2-native, psychrometric property calculations delegated PsychroLib appropriate.

title: Comfort model objects — comfort_model_pmv
text: Model objects capture fixed inputs used comfort layers. can reused across overlays, contours, zones, point states.

title: Comfort model objects — comfort_model_pmv
code: comfort_model_pmv(   tr = NULL,   vr = 0.1,   met = 1.2,   clo = 0.5,   wme = 0,   model = "7730-2005",   limit_inputs = FALSE,   round_output = FALSE )  comfort_model_set(   tr = NULL,   v = 0.1,   met = 1.2,   clo = 0.5,   wme = 0,   limit_inputs = FALSE,   body_surface_area = 1.8258,   p_atm = NULL,   position = c("standing", "sitting"),   round_output = FALSE )  comfort_model_adaptive(   t_running,   tr = NULL,   v = 0.1,   standard = c("ashrae55", "en16798"),   category = NULL,   limit_inputs = TRUE,   round_output = FALSE )  comfort_model_heat_index(   solar_exposure = 0,   limit_inputs = TRUE,   round_output = FALSE )

title: Comfort model objects — comfort_model_pmv
text: tr Mean radiant temperature. Defaults tdb. vr Relative air speed PMV. met Metabolic rate met. clo Clothing insulation clo. wme External work met. model PMV model/version label. Currently "7730-2005" implemented. limit_inputs TRUE, values outside model applicability range returned NA. round_output TRUE, round model outputs. Comfort plot layers use unrounded values default contours zones remain smooth. v Air speed SET adaptive comfort. body_surface_area Body surface area square meters SET. p_atm Atmospheric pressure Pa. position Body position, "standing" "sitting". t_running Running mean outdoor temperature. standard Adaptive comfort standard. category Adaptive comfort category. solar_exposure Relative solar exposure heat index, 0 1.

title: Comfort model objects — comfort_model_pmv
text: comfort model object.

title: Thermal comfort calculations — comfort_pmv
text: functions evaluate common comfort models without requiring Python runtime. Relative humidity always supplied percent. Temperature air-speed inputs follow units: SI uses degree C m/s; IP uses degree F ft/s.

title: Thermal comfort calculations — comfort_pmv
code: comfort_pmv(   tdb,   tr = tdb,   vr = 0.1,   rh,   met = 1.2,   clo = 0.5,   wme = 0,   units = c("SI", "IP"),   limit_inputs = TRUE,   round_output = TRUE )  comfort_set(   tdb,   tr = tdb,   v = 0.1,   rh,   met = 1.2,   clo = 0.5,   wme = 0,   units = c("SI", "IP"),   limit_inputs = TRUE,   round_output = TRUE,   body_surface_area = 1.8258,   p_atm = 101325,   position = c("standing", "sitting") )  comfort_adaptive(   tdb,   tr = tdb,   t_running,   v = 0.1,   standard = c("ashrae55", "en16798"),   category = NULL,   units = c("SI", "IP"),   limit_inputs = TRUE,   round_output = TRUE )  comfort_heat_index(   tdb,   rh,   solar_exposure = 0,   units = c("SI", "IP"),   limit_inputs = TRUE,   round_output = TRUE )

title: Thermal comfort calculations — comfort_pmv

title: Draw psychrometric state points or process lines — geom_psychro_process
code: process <- data.frame(     state = c("outdoor", "mixed", "supply", "room"),     tdb = c(18, 23, 28, 31),     relhum = c(70, 55, 45, 55) )  # Draw state points. Map exactly one psychrometric property with tdb. ggpsychro(tdb_lim = c(0, 40), hum_lim = c(0, 25)) +     stat_psychro_state(         aes(tdb = tdb, relhum = relhum, colour = state),         data = process     )   # Draw the same states as a process line. ggpsychro(tdb_lim = c(0, 40), hum_lim = c(0, 25)) +     geom_psychro_process(         aes(tdb = tdb, relhum = relhum),         data = process,         linewidth = 1,         arrow = grid::arrow(length = grid::unit(0.08, "inches"))     ) +     stat_psychro_state(         aes(tdb = tdb, relhum = relhum),         data = process,         size = 2     )

title: Draw psychrometric zones — geom_psychro_zone
text: Supported zone types : "dbt-rh": tdb_min, tdb_max, relhum_min, relhum_max "enthalpy-rh": enthalpy_min, enthalpy_max, relhum_min, relhum_max "specvol-rh" "volume-rh": specvol_min, specvol_max, relhum_min, relhum_max "dbt-wmax": tdb_min, tdb_max, humratio_max, optional humratio_min "xy-points": tdb, humratio, optional group relhum values supplied percent. humratio values supplied chart display units: g/kg SI gr/lb IP.

title: Draw psychrometric zones — geom_psychro_zone
code: comfort <- data.frame(     name = c("cool", "warm"),     tdb_min = c(20, 24),     tdb_max = c(26, 32),     relhum_min = c(35, 45),     relhum_max = c(60, 75) )  ggpsychro(tdb_lim = c(0, 40), hum_lim = c(0, 25)) +     geom_psychro_zone(         aes(tdb_min = tdb_min, tdb_max = tdb_max,             relhum_min = relhum_min, relhum_max = relhum_max,             fill = name),         data = comfort,         type = "dbt-rh",         alpha = 0.28,         colour = NA     )   # Dry-bulb range with humidity-ratio limits. humidity_cap <- data.frame(     tdb_min = 10,     tdb_max = 34,     humratio_min = 4,     humratio_max = 12 ) ggpsychro(tdb_lim = c(0, 40), hum_lim = c(0, 25)) +     geom_psychro_zone(         aes(tdb_min = tdb_min, tdb_max = tdb_max,             humratio_min = humratio_min, humratio_max = humratio_max),         data = humidity_cap,         type = "dbt-wmax",         alpha = 0.25     )   # Property-bounded zones. enthalpy_zone <- data.frame(     enthalpy_min = 40000,     enthalpy_max = 65000,     relhum_min = 30,     relhum_max = 80 ) ggpsychro(tdb_lim = c(0, 40), hum_lim = c(0, 25)) +     geom_psychro_zone(         aes(enthalpy_min = enthalpy_min, enthalpy_max = enthalpy_max,             relhum_min = relhum_min, relhum_max = relhum_max),         data = enthalpy_zone,         type = "enthalpy-rh",         alpha = 0.25     )   # Draw an already-specified polygon in chart display units. polygon_zone <- data.frame(     tdb = c(20, 26, 28),     humratio = c(6, 8, 6) ) ggpsychro(tdb_lim = c(0, 40), hum_lim = c(0, 25)) +     geom_psychro_zone(         aes(tdb = tdb, humratio = humratio),         data = polygon_zone,         type = "xy-points",         alpha = 0.25     )

title: ggpsychro 0.0.0.9000
text: Added Heat Index overlays, Givoni bioclimatic strategy zones, per-zone styling controls Givoni overlays. (#24) Added optional contour labels geom_comfort_contour(). (#26) Clarified SET adaptive comfort overlay examples comfort overlays article. (#25) Added README acknowledgements external psychrometric-chart references refined SET/adaptive comfort overlay examples. (#23) Added README pkgdown documentation comfort overlays psychrometric protractor. (#22) Added ASHRAE-style psychrometric protractor sensible heat ratio heat-moisture-ratio guides, including Mollier rotation support, overall scaling, independent mask-area margins. (#21) Added thermal comfort calculations comfort overlay layers PMV/PPD, SET, adaptive comfort, PMV curves, PMV-based ASHRAE 55 / EN 15251 comfort zones, point-state comfort metrics. (#20) Removed ambiguous specvol-rh zone example reference documentation. (#19) Clipped geom_psychro_tile() bodies saturation curve tiles stay inside valid psychrometric region. (#17) Added runnable core reference examples psychrometric stats, grid helpers, state/process layers, zones, explicit coordinates. (#18) Added website-pkgdown articles shortened README. (#15) Added psychrometric tile bins. (#14) Added psychrometric chart presets. (#12) Added psychrometric zones process layers. (#13) Rendered psychrometric grid labels. (#11) Revived dual psychrometric grid API. (#10)
